hub.seoTitle: Facebook Tools — Page Analyzer, Content Downloader & Content Browser
hub.sectionSub: dedicated tools for Facebook page and group analysis, content saving, content browsing, and activity tracking.
hub.ctaSub: Provide any public Facebook profile or page name to generate comprehensive analytics and insights.

faqs.question: Can someone see if you view their Facebook profile?
faqs.answer: No. Facebook does not notify users when someone views their profile. Any app or service claiming otherwise is not legitimate.

faqs.question: Does Facebook still have its own analytics tool?
faqs.answer: Facebook retired its standalone Analytics tool in 2021. Page insights are now available through Meta Business Suite. Third-party tools like IGStoryPeek provide additional analytics for public pages.

faqs.question: How do you view a public Facebook page without logging in?
faqs.answer: Many public Facebook pages are accessible without an account. IGStoryPeek allows you to search and analyze public Facebook pages and profiles without logging in to Facebook.

faqs.question: What happened to Facebook Analytics after it was retired?
faqs.answer: Facebook Analytics was retired on June 30, 2021. Its features were absorbed into Meta Business Suite and Ads Manager. Third-party tools now fill the gap for external page analysis.

faqs.question: Can you track competitors' Facebook pages with analytics tools?
faqs.answer: Yes. Tools like IGStoryPeek let you monitor public Facebook pages, track engagement metrics, and compare performance — all without needing access to the page's own insights.

faqs.question: What metrics should you track on a Facebook business page?
faqs.answer: Important Facebook page metrics include page likes, post reach, engagement rate, click-through rate, video views, and follower growth. Tracking these helps measure content effectiveness.
